Welcome to Ad Lab, a digital marketing agency specialising in Magento eCommerce website design, development and eCommerce marketing strategies.

We understand that attempting to establish your brand and expand your business can be time-consuming at best, and entirely fruitless at worst. Thats why we pride ourselves on guiding you every step of the way.

Our team of professionals can provide your company with a new website, boost your online presence and exposure via a combination of paid and organic content, and achieve positive returns by measuring your transactional data to facilitate consistent optimisations.

As certified Google partners, were also able to attach your product feed to various prominent marketplaces, increasing conversion rates exponentially over the course of several months.  

Our proven expertise as certified Magento developers also allows us to migrate your existing systems and products to another platform, or integrate entirely new and bespoke features onto your new website.

If your business is equipped to handle the growth we can provide, half the battle is already won. All you need to do is tell us: how high do you want to raise the bar? 

If you would like to know more about the services we provide, as well as how we can expand your company, give us a call today. Alternatively, you can reach us via email at any time.

Charlie Puffin Digital is a digital marketing agency based in Hitchin, Hertfordshire.

We founded Charlie Puffin Digital in August 2020, right in the middle of the coronavirus pandemic. With one aim:

To help SMEs deliver real business results through social media.

Not doing social media because everyone else is or because its the right thing to do, but working with SMEs to identify the results they want, then developing an approach to deliver those results through the power of social media.

For too long SMEs have either ignored, played at social media in-house, or spent a lot of money with agencies who create and share content often with little thought to the business objectives.

Well, were here to do things a little differently, starting with our beliefs about Social Media;

 It can help unlock business growth
 It doesnt need to be complicated or overly expensive
 The results it delivers can, and should be, measured

So, if youd like to grow your customer base and business through social media, get in touch

We are Uber. The go-getters. The kind of people who are relentless about our mission to help people go anywhere and get anything and earn their way. Movement is what we power. Its our lifeblood. It runs through our veins. Its what gets us out of bed each morning. It pushes us to constantly reimagine how we can move better. For you. For all the places you want to go. For all the things you want to get. For all the ways you want to earn. Across the entire world. In real time. At the incredible speed of now.

The idea for Uber was born on a snowy night in Paris in 2008, and ever since then our DNA of reimagination and reinvention carries on. Weve grown into a global platform powering flexible earnings and the movement of people and things in ever expanding ways. Weve gone from connecting rides on 4 wheels to 2 wheels to 18-wheel freight deliveries. From takeout meals to daily essentials to prescription drugs to just about anything you need at any time and earning your way. From drivers with background checks to real-time verification, safety is a top priority every single day. At Uber, the pursuit of reimagination is never finished, never stops, and is always just beginning

Modern Nature was established in 2013, when our founder Bessie Rollins decided to create a naturally tasty version of the stevia sweeteners she was using in order to combat the rare genetic condition she was suffering from.

By replacing some of the sugar in her diet with stevia, Vanessa found that she was able to dramatically improve her health and wellbeing without compromising on the sweet stuff. The only problem was that until now, people choosing to adopt a low sugar lifestyle had to use artificial sweeteners or stevia formulas that were full of artificial additives. 

After two years of careful tweaking, we at Modern Nature have found a way to bring the benefits of stevia to you in the form of Modern Natures THE LIQUID STEVIA SWEETENER. 

Because our product is in a liquid state, it doesnt need to use the bulking powders that youll find in other stevia products, and it is suitable to be used with every kind of lifestyle. As it is great for baking, drinks and dressings, it can be used just like sugar. However, as THE LIQUID STEVIA SWEETENER contains no calories at all, it has no effect on your blood sugar levels and does not affect your dental health, making it a superior alternative to sugar.

Give our liquid stevia a try  theres only sugar to lose and a whole lot of taste to gain!

If you want to ask any questions about our product, please email us at info@modernnature.co.uk

Our purpose as a business is to Make Things Better, our spaces, our food & drink, but most
importantly - our people and the communities in which we serve.

Since for ever the British cafe has been an iconic institution, a place to celebrate individuality,
innovation, community and participation. However over the last few decades they have been under
attack by the chain invasions threatening the British high street with a deluge of boring corporate
soullessness.

Each of our cafes is designed to be a one off, a cherished local. We want to remain a cafe to be
discovered, recommended and returned to. We do this not only by ensuring no two cafes look or feel
the same, but by ensuring they have unique personalities, driven by our people. This is a place
where youre accepted for who you are.

Classically British (despite the name) were on a mission to serve simply and well-prepared food and
drinks made with great ingredients, skill and love. Ethically sourced, affordable, feel-good, great
value, healthy and excellent quality - made onsite everyday.

As we grow we are determined to get better (we call this scale vs soul) refusing to accept that you have
to compromise our values, the stuff that really matters to us, as we expand.

All of this we do because we want to be the epic soulful cafe chain we think the world needs.

Like the sound of this and want to join the team? See our latest vacancies on our Leisure Jobs page: www.leisurejobs.com/minisites/boston-tea-party

Lolas Cupcakes began back in 2006 when two friends, Victoria Jossel and Romy Lewis, started things the good old fashioned way. Originally taking orders in their bedrooms and baking in their parents kitchens, the orders soon started flooding in and they had to move production to a bakery in Primrose Hill.

With their love for baking and passion for the finest, freshest ingredients the company grew from strength to strength. Lolas opened concessions in Harrods, Selfridges and Topshop, a flagship in Mayfair, an online store and has worked hard to produce Londons best cupcakes.  

Victoria and Romy moved on to other projects, but Lolas continues to grow in team and spirit. Now with twenty stores across London, Lolas handcrafts cakes and cupcakes for the city daily.

In each of our stores, from Chancery Lane Station to Canary Wharf and even Selfridges Birmingham, our cupcake specialists are on hand to advise and to give you a taste of our classic flavours as well as our seasonal new discoveries.

Our sponges rise with the sunrise and our bakers and decorators handcraft our cakes and cupcakes using only the finest ingredients.  Fresh every day, always, Lolas Cupcakes brings a smile of sweetness to people every single day

Essex-born and London-based, were a burger truck turned raved-about restaurant in the heart of Shoreditch. To find the perfect patty, we drew inspiration from burger joints all over the world, from London to Miami, Vegas to LA, Berlin to NYC. So we know a good burger when we bite one.

The Fine Cheese Co. is a maturer, wholesaler, retailer and exporter of artisan British cheeses, most of which are unpasteurised and all of which are traditionally made.

We are based in historic Bath, founded by the Romans and famous for its beautiful Georgian architecture - alongside Venice the only World Heritage city in Europe.

Bath is set in the heart of the English West Country, the home of the most famous of British cheeses: Cheddar.

We have been cheese specialists for twenty years, and from the start we have nurtured and supported the cream of British cheese-makers. We stock over a hundred hand-made British cheeses: from rediscovered gems such as Devon Oke, a hard but creamy cows milk cheese made to the original seventeenth century recipe, to Lord of the Hundreds, an unpasteurised mature sheeps milk cheese made in the style of an Italian Pecorino.

The Fine Cheese Co. has been owned and run from the beginning by Ann-Marie Dyas. A judge at both the British and World Cheese Awards, Ann-Marie is committed to the survival of traditional cheese-making, and to introducing farmhouse cheeses to as wide a public as possible.

However, our love of cheese extends beyond these shores. We also import artisan cheeses from France, Italy, Spain and Holland. (Sometimes the only way to get the Dutch to part with their finest aged Gouda is to go over and get it.)

Throughout our history, The Fine Cheese Co. has sought, cared for and loved hand-made cheese. We have also made it our business to know what flatters it and makes it a new experience. Our dedicated and unique ranges of Crackers (made by our sister Company, Artisan Biscuits), Fruits, Whole Fruits, Chutneys, and English Pickles for Cheese are the result.

We are a strong supporter of the independent retailer  after all we are an independent retailer ourselves  and none of The Fine Cheese Co. ranges of products will ever be sold in a British supermarket
organizationSpecialities: Cheese,Crackers for Cheese,Biscuits for Cheese,Charcuterie,Wine,Whole Fruits,Fruits,English Pickles,Chutneys,and Artisan food

Established in London in 2000, Newby employs the very highest standards found in tea production today. Enjoyed in select establishments around the world, including 5-star hotels, award-winning restaurants, cultural venues and stately homes, Newby products can also be found in retail outlets such as Waitrose and Sainsburys, and are sold globally online via newbyteas.com.

Its not just the quality of our fine teas which sets us apart. Newbys 5-star client service has evolved to cater to a detail-oriented and prestigious customer base. Our in-house tea training and collaborative efforts to create unique tea menus and food pairings have enhanced a multitude of fine dining, conferencing and banqueting establishments, and many blue-chip companies and businesses enjoy a superb reputation through serving Newby.

The guiding principles on which the company was founded still hold true to this day. We blend our teas using the very best leaves, we use these leaves only to create our own teas, and our founder, Mr Sethia, still personally tastes and approves all teas bearing the Newby name. 

Newby is the only tea company to have its own dedicated preservation and packing facility, based in Kolkata in the heart of the major tea-growing regions. Our factory is AA-rated for food safety by BRC Global Standards and is comprised of temperature-controlled preservation rooms to ensure every variety of tea is stored in optimal conditions. 

We protect our teas in specially designed multi-layer packaging for extra freshness, taking the leaves from garden to pack in as little as three months - a quarter of the time of many tea companies. 

Our headquarters in London, England, is supported by a growing network of distribution partners and international offices, including those in Russia, USA, Switzerland, UAE, India, Singapore and the Netherlands.

To find out more visit newbyteas.com
organizationSpecialities: Classic Caddies,Silken Pyramids,Loose Leaf Tea,Tea Bags,Black Tea,White Tea,Green Tea,Oolong Tea,Herbal,Flavoured Tea,and Tea Gifts

organizationDescription:
At Purition we set out to create honest, naturally nutritious small meals to support your daily, optimal health ambitions.
In a marketplace crammed full of refined, ultra-processed supplements pretending to be food in the form of, diet shakes, protein powders and so-called complete powdered meals packed with ultra-processed, non-food, ingredients like gums, starches, artificial sweeteners, emulsifiers, preservatives, anti-caking agents, synthetic vitamins and minerals; Purition takes a welcome stand for real food and healthier living routines.
Each minimally processed recipe made at our purpose-built Shropshire facility is a simple yet well-balanced blend of 70% natural wholefoods (seeds & nuts) mixed with the finest European vegetarian or vegan protein.
Purition is a vocal champion for healthy food that doesnt need cooking or a knife and fork; an ambitious British business that shows convenience can be naturally nutritious and delicious and that ultra processed, hyper palatable supplements providing only empty calories low-grade ingredients of unspecified origin serve only the companies making them, offering customers nothing but powder and a slick marketing message. These junk powders are not only unhealthy they are also completely unnecessary and raise important safety questions over the long-term consumption of so much non-food ingredients, especially when real whole foods are made just as convenient and pack more usable and useful nutrition.   In short, Purition works exclusively with real food to support: healthier eating habits, increased protein needs, active lifestyles, long-term sustainable weight loss and performance
organizationSpecialities: Nutrition,Gluten Free,Food,Weight-loss,Meal Replacements,Protein,Manufacturing,Shakes,and Wholefoods

Lolas Cupcakes began back in 2006 when two friends, Victoria Jossel and Romy Lewis, started things the good old fashioned way. Originally taking orders in their bedrooms and baking in their parents kitchens, the orders soon started flooding in and they had to move production to a bakery in Primrose Hill.

With their love for baking and passion for the finest, freshest ingredients the company grew from strength to strength. Lolas opened concessions in Harrods, Selfridges and Topshop, a flagship in Mayfair, an online store and has worked hard to produce Londons best cupcakes.  

Victoria and Romy moved on to other projects, but Lolas continues to grow in team and spirit. Now with twenty stores across London, Lolas handcrafts cakes and cupcakes for the city daily.

In each of our stores, from Chancery Lane Station to Canary Wharf and even Selfridges Birmingham, our cupcake specialists are on hand to advise and to give you a taste of our classic flavours as well as our seasonal new discoveries.

Somebody is a full service digital agency with a one track mind  getting you customers, not just clicks. 

We specialise in these areas:

* Conversion Rate Optimisation (CRO) where we help clients ensure that website visitors end up doing what you want them to do. Typically improving lead generation or ecommerce conversion rates and increasing average order values. However it can also include improving micro conversions, such as email newsletter sign ups or social follows.
* Quality traffic generation - typically with a tight ROI or cost per acquisition target. More often than not the programmes are international and involves full translation and localisation management. Programmes include:
    * PPC
    * Google & Amazon Shopping
    * Programatic display
    * Paid Social though Linkedin, Facebook, Instagram, Twitter, Pinterest etc
    * SEO
* Analytics which, besides being a service in its own right, is the foundation of all other programmes - without exactly knowing how visitors are behaving by channel, device, age, gender etc. it is impossible to fully optimise a websites performance. Analytics tend to be a foundation for everything we do, but it is also a programme in its own right, where we create easy to understand dashboards so you quickly can get a true view of performance and drill down into specific areas in case you need more detail

1.	Define We define your needs by understanding your objectives and auditing your current performance.

We like to think of ourselves as a customer journey agency, delivering the ultimate user journey across all digital marketing. Our ethos is to become Your Partner in DigitalTM, instilled in everything we do. It defines our brand and delivers outstanding results that personify this motto. 

Since launching in 2005, we have successfully developed and grown our unique omni-channel approach and worked with leading organisations across a spectrum of sectors. From finance to e-commerce, education to entertainment, weve created some of the UKs most compelling and innovative projects, transforming the digital presence of AA, Money Supermarket, Redrow, Clogau, Centre of Excellence & more. 

We work with our clients as an extension of their internal teams, taking a partner-centric approach that delivers returns over and beyond expectations. This is reflected in everything from the way we collaborate on creative ideas to implementing their digital projects. 

Our partners performance has been central to the accolades weve secured, named by The Drum as the best agency in the UK for client satisfaction in 2021. Weve also been recognised as a top UK B2B company for 4 years running by the world-leading reviews and ratings platform, Clutch. These are alongside accolades from AAAwards, Ad World Masters, and ThreeBestRated. 

Weve honed our approach over many years, always staying ahead of the demands, trends and expectations of the digital marketing industry. organizationDescription:
Missing out on activities you love because of back, hip, knee or shoulder pain?
 Whether its gardening, running, walking, or simply enjoying time with family, pain and stiffness can quickly take away freedom and confidenceespecially after 50.
At Urban Body, we help people aged 50+ stay active, mobile, and pain-free with a personalised plan that fits their lifestyle and goals. No more short-term fixes or masking symptoms. Just real results and long-term solutions.
What our clients often struggle with:
  Ongoing pain that limits movement and daily enjoyment
  Frustration with treatments that dont last
  Feeling like no one is listening or understanding their goals
Our approach is different. We treat the person before the patient. We take time to build trust, understand what matters most, and create a clear path back to the life they want.
Using a multi-therapist model, each client works with two expert practitionersdrawing on skills in physiotherapy, sports therapy, exercise rehab, acupuncture, orthotics and massageto treat the root cause of the problem.
We work to achieve results within a defined time frame, with every treatment tailored to the individuals body, needs, and goals.
What clients are saying:
 What a team! Luke and Adam have both been amazing. So knowledgeable, caring, and fabulous therapists. Really made a wonderful difference to my discomfort and mobility.  Mel Delaney
Suffered a knee injury at 55 that had me thinking Id have to give up running. From the first session, Luke put those fears behind me. I now feel stronger and Im looking forward to running a marathon in 4 months!  Michael Beck
Ready to get back to doing what you love?
  Contact Urban Body for a free assessment and find out if were the right fit to help.
 Call: 0121 270 6045

Here at Farrow & Ball, were dedicated to creating a range of richly pigmented paints and handcrafted wallpapers, using only the highest-quality ingredients.

Still rooted in Dorset, England, our palette is available in a full range of high-performance interior and exterior finishes. Deepening from subtle neutrals to dark inky hues, our paint is blended with up to five pigments for an extraordinary depth of colour.

Its also here that we craft each roll of our wallpaper with Farrow & Ball paint and responsibly sourced paper. Our traditional printing methods give an exquisitely tactile, almost three-dimensional finish as you seamlessly combine paint and paper in the same space.

It began in 1946 with local pioneers John Farrow and Richard Ball. Our founders were groundbreaking in their pursuit of only the finest quality ingredients. From then on, our laboratory forged cutting-edge methods for testing the exceptional colour and performance of our paint finishes. 

Today the unique Farrow & Ball look transforms modern and traditional homes, large and small, inside and out, around the world. Even with more than 60 showrooms worldwide and a global network of stockists, weve never forgotten our local Dorset roots

The company works with its customers and partners on energy systems for the future, thus supporting the transition to a more sustainable world. With its portfolio of products, solutions and services, Siemens Energy covers almost the entire energy value chain  from power generation and transmission to storage. The portfolio includes conventional and renewable energy technology, such as gas and steam turbines, hybrid power plants operated with hydrogen, and power generators and transformers. A majority stake in the wind power subsidiary Siemens Gamesa Renewable Energy (SGRE) makes Siemens Energy a global market leader for renewable energies. An estimated one-sixth of the electricity generated worldwide is based on technologies from Siemens Energy. Siemens Energy employs 98,000 people worldwide in more than 90 countries and generated revenue of around 31 billion in fiscal year 2023.

Saras Fix is an independent electronics repair business based in Bloomsbury, Central London, with a strong focus on sustainability, affordability, and community engagement.

With over a decade of experience, we specialise in the repair of laptops, smartphones, tablets, cameras, and other handheld electronic devices. Our goal is not only to provide high-quality, reliable repairs but also to extend the lifespan of technology and reduce the growing problem of electronic waste.

What sets Saras Fix apart is our commitment to the Right to Repair movement. We believe that consumers should have the ability, access, and confidence to repair their devices, rather than be forced to replace them. This belief is reflected in our day-to-day services and in our outreach work.

Alongside our repair business, we actively organise and participate in free Repair Cafes across London  including events at universities such as UCL and Imperial College, libraries, and community centres. At these events, we offer free repairs, guidance, and demonstrations, helping people better understand their devices and encouraging a hands-on approach to maintenance and repair.

Weve proudly worked with and earned the trust of major institutions like Great Ormond Street Hospital, UCL, and local councils, and our work has been recognised by BBC, ITV, and The Guardian. With over 500 five-star reviews, Saras Fix is widely trusted for its technical expertise, transparency, and customer care.

We also contribute to the circular economy by promoting reuse, refurbishing components, and offering training opportunities to young people and aspiring technicians.

Whether youre a student, a business, or a member of the public, Saras Fix is here to make repair accessible, sustainable, and empowering  because fixing is better than throwing away

organizationDescription:
Pragmatically Pretentious; it may sound silly (OK, it does sound silly), but theres more to it than that. 

Basically, were trying to show that you can represent who you are without having to pay the added price for someone elses questionable manufacturing process. Which is why all of our products are WRAP (Worldwide Responsible Accredited Production) certified. That means that not only is all production done in ways which reduce its impact on the planet, but that its also done with a socially responsible approach as well. So all of our materials are organic, from the cotton to the ink, and everyone involved in the manufacturing process is paid a fair wage and ensured a safe working environment.

Not that any of this should be special, because its how things should be done in the first place. When you think about it, its just the pragmatic way of doing things

Power Nations Core Values
ATHLETE FIRST  we put the athlete first in everything that we do. As a result, all of our products are designed to enhance performance and support you physically and mentally to reach your full potential.

 We research the latest trends, both high-street and high-end, to offer you the most fashion-forward pieces of the season.

organizationDescription:
Serena Bute first made a name for herself back in the 80s when she started making womens shirts under her first label, Wendell and Howes. In the late 80s she moved to Jamaica, where she spent the next 10 years. There was very little to buy in terms of kids clothes and so she started her own childrens wear label.
 
Serena moved back to London in the mid 90s with her 2 children and from the kitchen of her house in Shepherds Bush, she started Anonymous by Ross + Bute. She was first inspired by some thermal vests that she came across whilst styling a fashion shoot. The label evolved into a highly feminine, ageless womenswear brand specialising in knitwear.
 
After deciding to close Ross and Bute, Serena took a few years out to spend time with her family. However, she continued to make samples inspired by her own personal wardrobe, she made crepe suits which led on to trousers, all specialising in ease and comfort for people with hectic lives and those that are always on the move.
 
Finally, in 2015, Serena established her self-titled brand. The capsule collection of wide leg joggers with grosgrain detailing offers the perfect building blocks for a comfortable wardrobe. Appropriate for women of all ages, the trousers represent Serenas personal ethos: stylish, comfortable, easy to wear and suitable through day to night
organizationSpecialities: Womens Clothing,100% Silk Clothing,Womens Apparel,Womens Trousers,and Womens Shirts

organizationDescription:
The British Fashion Council (BFC) is a notforprofit organisation empowering the future of British fashion. Since 1983, it has united industry expertise and resources to strengthen the UKs position as a global creative leader, supported by patrons, commercial partners and government funding.

The BFC showcases British design excellence on the world stage, London Fashion Week presents innovative, genderinclusive collections each February and September. While LONDON show ROOMS takes emerging British designers to Paris, opening the doors to international markets. The BFC also organises, The Fashion Awards, celebrating industry innovation and global impact.

Through the BFC Foundation, we support the future growth and success of the British fashion industry by focusing on education, grant-giving, and business mentoring through initiatives such as BFC NEWGEN, the BFC/GQ Designer Fashion Fund, BFC/Vogue Designer Fashion Fund and the BFC Colleges Council, the BFC supports designers at every stage of their journey, nurturing the next generation and shaping the future of British fashion

organizationDescription:
Skippers began in early 2013 by former fish merchant Steve 
Moore.  Skippers is situated in Grimsby, known for its rich fishing heritage 
and status as Europes Food Town. Steve has a long history with the local fish industry and saw an opportunity to produce high-quality natural dog treats.

Here at Skippers, we have 80 years of combined experience in the 
fishing industry. Our treats are low-fat natural fish-based treats 
that act as an everyday treat and as a treat to help clean a dogs 
teeth, protect gums, prevent stiff joints, etc.

From experience built over years and years, we have created 
relations with fish merchants that provide us with the best quality 
fish. It is very important to us where we source our fish because we want 
to provide dogs with the best quality treats. A large percentage 
of our fish comes from zone 27 in the Atlantic ocean, and these 
zones are responsibly monitored by the Marine Stewardship 
Council (MSC).

Our main lines of dog treats consist of Cod or Wolf Fish. Our Cod 
and Wolf Fish are sourced in zone 27 in the Atlantic Ocean, and 
our Salmon are sourced from 2 locations; Scotland and zone 67 
in the Pacific Ocean. Sprats are another main fish species in our 
range, sourced from zone 27 III in the Atlantic ocean

Serena Bute first made a name for herself back in the 80s when she started making womens shirts under her first label, Wendell and Howes. In the late 80s she moved to Jamaica, where she spent the next 10 years. There was very little to buy in terms of kids clothes and so she started her own childrens wear label.
 
Serena moved back to London in the mid 90s with her 2 children and from the kitchen of her house in Shepherds Bush, she started Anonymous by Ross + Bute. She was first inspired by some thermal vests that she came across whilst styling a fashion shoot. The label evolved into a highly feminine, ageless womenswear brand specialising in knitwear.
 
After deciding to close Ross and Bute, Serena took a few years out to spend time with her family. However, she continued to make samples inspired by her own personal wardrobe, she made crepe suits which led on to trousers, all specialising in ease and comfort for people with hectic lives and those that are always on the move.
 
Finally, in 2015, Serena established her self-titled brand. The capsule collection of wide leg joggers with grosgrain detailing offers the perfect building blocks for a comfortable wardrobe. Appropriate for women of all ages, the trousers represent Serenas personal ethos: stylish, comfortable, easy to wear and suitable through day to night

organizationDescription:
Here at Farrow & Ball, were dedicated to creating a range of richly pigmented paints and handcrafted wallpapers, using only the highest-quality ingredients.

Still rooted in Dorset, England, our palette is available in a full range of high-performance interior and exterior finishes. Deepening from subtle neutrals to dark inky hues, our paint is blended with up to five pigments for an extraordinary depth of colour.

Its also here that we craft each roll of our wallpaper with Farrow & Ball paint and responsibly sourced paper. Our traditional printing methods give an exquisitely tactile, almost three-dimensional finish as you seamlessly combine paint and paper in the same space.

It began in 1946 with local pioneers John Farrow and Richard Ball. Our founders were groundbreaking in their pursuit of only the finest quality ingredients. From then on, our laboratory forged cutting-edge methods for testing the exceptional colour and performance of our paint finishes. 

Today the unique Farrow & Ball look transforms modern and traditional homes, large and small, inside and out, around the world. Even with more than 60 showrooms worldwide and a global network of stockists, weve never forgotten our local Dorset roots
organizationSpecialities: Paint,Handcrafted wallpaper,and Colour Consultancy

organizationDescription:
At viwiMX we focus on growth for software as a service (SaaS) and technology companies alike.

We tailor our approach completely to the business need and make sure that you are served at the highest level, allowing you to maximise ROI and focus on the right kind of automation that drives sales and increases revenue.

We are a group of people passionate about all things digital marketing. We came together to develop a full-scale solution for SaaS businesses tackling digital marketing challenges. In our efforts to enable teams and agencies to identify and pursue digital marketing opportunities in their niche, weve developed a marketing intelligence platform suite.

At one point, we became aware that our goal was set very high. However, after countless sleepless nights and lots of caffeine, we came to a solution. Our intelligent marketing platform is now available to businesses of all sizes that want to leverage the benefits of digital marketing and identify the best performing digital channels.

We dont believe in marketing based on a hunch or previous experience. Thats why we developed tools that allow our clients to track leads to their source, monitor marketing strategy efficiency, discover what competition is doing, and get actionable insights from reports.

The viwiMX team is proud to present to you a marketing platform able to turn you into a digital marketing guru. Whether you need to keep track of your keyword rankings, discover sections of your website that need improvement, or create a high-converting sales funnel, you can now do it all in one place.

With a full-blown easy-to-use dashboard, you can easily stay on top of your marketing game and be one step ahead of the competition

Jempsons is an independent family owned business established in Peasmarsh, East Sussex in June 1935 by George Thomas Jempson.

We currently operate supermarkets, convenience stores, Post Office counters, Pharmacy, petrol station and cafes in and around East Sussex. 

Jempsons has been a proud member of Nisa since 1982 and has been the recipient of many awards and accolades including the coveted Independent Retailer of the Year.

We are keen advocators of the Keep Sunday Special campaign and pride ourselves with our relationships with customers and suppliers. 

Our mission statement:
Since 1935 Jempsons has sought to promote traditional, homemade foods to the public. 

Built on honesty and good value, our intention is to lead the retail food industry in terms of innovation, design and the very best in superb quality fresh foods.

Our mission is to become the most prestigious food retailer in the UK offering the consumer the very highest standards in customer service and some of the finest foods from all over the world and around the corner.

This is our guarantee. This is our Mission.

We are committed to helping and supporting the local community in which we are proud to operate stores, the Jempson Foundation charitable trust was set up with the sole purpose to fulfil this ambition. 

Jempsons family values, everyday value.

Proudly serving Peasmarsh and East Sussex since 1935

Meanwhile, the Labelled collection is a line up of intuitively-designed pieces  from zippable pockets to activity-specific bags  which celebrate the art of organisation.

Committed to creating responsibly, the brand constantly strives to innovate to reduce its impact on the earth and drive education and discussion around sustainability, paying heed to the mantra, progress, not perfection. organizationDescription:
Passenger is a responsible outdoor clothing brand with a mission to inspire meaningful escapism. Getting outside matters, disconnecting to reconnect, unplugging to recharge. We believe that finding escapism in our passions, our connections and in nature recharges us, bringing balance and wellbeing.

We want to inspire people to find their own paths, roam further and embrace their own journey, whatever that might be. Stories of escapism are woven into the fabric of our products and each design tells a different one, the smell of a campfire on a beanie or the salt-water stain on a favourite tee. We design all our products with this in mind, hoping another tale will be made with each one.

We know that by inspiring people to spend more time outdoors and offering a product that enables them to do that easily, people will fall in love with the outdoors, the places they play. And in turn will want to protect those places, look after them and advocate for them. This is why our Plant & Protect programme is so important to us. We plant a tree for every order and protect acres and acres of rainforest every month. Always have, always will.

Designed To Wander, Made To Roam

organizationDescription:
For those who share our love of the sea.
Proudly #BCorp


It started in 2003 in a flat above a surf shop with an innovative fleece that was designed to keep out chill winds and warm up cold bodies. Since these early days, driven by those who have jumped on board, supported by our customers and suppliers, the brand and product has widened beyond these original beginnings. Yet from our cliff top workshop in St. Agnes, we stay true to our original design ethos of functionality and sustainability brought to life in an understated style and identity, always remaining committed to product, environment and people.

The name of one of the most rugged headlands that bravely bears the full brunt of the North Atlantic gales, and a former shipping forecast area, Finisterre literally means end of the earth or lands end. The place where land ends has intrigued man for centuries, fostering both a real and a romantic relationship with the sea; a relationship that runs deep in our narrative.

Since day one we have taken a pioneering approach to making better and more sustainable product, challenging and innovating, seeking alternatives to what has gone before. Were committed to making informed decisions about our impact on the environment and are constantly trying to push boundaries to make the best product we can. In our eyes this is gear that will be with you the longest; and the longer you have it, the more attached to it you become.

For all media requests regarding the Finisterre brand, products and environmental efforts, please contact: press@finisterre.com

organizationDescription:
As of October 2023, 4.95 billion individuals, representing 61.4 percent of the global population were active on social media platforms.

With so many social platforms, its important to consider which ones are going to add real value to your business before you begin to create brand accounts.
 
Why be picky when you could go all in? Because it depends on your brands existing reputation and reach. If youre a recognised brand, you should participate in a wide range of social platforms. If youre a small brand, or just getting started it may make sense to focus on a couple of key strategic platforms, rather than spreading yourself too thin.

Having worked as a freelance social media marketer since 2017, Claire is able to advise and develop proven strategies to grow social channels both organically and through paid campaigns. Claire has two design degrees, a B.A. (Hons) first class and a Masters in design, giving her the ability to not only problem solve, but to also produce creative assets for your social profiles.

Industry experience  Professional services, recruitment, green energy, food and drink, health, beauty, fitness and software

The Chelsea Gardener opened in Sydney Street in 1984 and was established by the second generation of the Fenwick family. In the late 1930s, the family had established several nurseries in and around Essex and it was here that they developed their knowledge and passion for outdoor living. Realising that London needed a one-stop shop to help people create the best of their outdoor space, no matter how limited in size, we took over the large site that was once part of The Brompton Hospital.

The flexible and large space at The Chelsea Gardener lends itself well as a venue for parties and launches. Guests enjoy a central London venue yet are transported by the surroundings to a well-maintained garden or stunning and intimate orangery, a million miles away from the hustle and bustle of city life. This is a truly unique space for private drinks parties, sit-down dinners, corporate events, fashion and art shows, etc. which can be enjoyed all year round.

The Chelsea Gardener today remains privately owned and the family continues to ensure that their original values are upheld: To provide high quality, inspiring products and plants alongside excellent customer service, ensuring that The Chelsea Gardener is a source and inspiration for those who seek imaginative new ideas for their outdoor living

organizationDescription:
BOXPARKTM

BOXPARK brings communities together by offering the best in street food, drinks, and entertainment under one roof. Since pioneering the use of shipping containers in East London, BOXPARK has transformed pop-up dining and retail into a lasting cultural movement.

Founded by Roger Wade in 2011, BOXPARK blends container architecture, independent retail, and a strong community focus to create unmatched cultural destinations. Its signature Eat.Drink.Play philosophy has helped shape modern British culture.

Over the years, BOXPARK has expanded to a portfolio of vibrant venues across the UK, including its flagship site in Shoreditch and locations in Croydon, Wembley, Camden, and Liverpool.

In 2025, BOXPARK entered an exciting new chapter with the launch of two pioneering concepts. BOXHALL, a premium food hall, opened within a historic arcade in the heart of Liverpool Streetoffering curated dining experiences, two bars, and a rotating, chef-led kitchen showcasing the best of Londons food scene.

That same year, BOXPARK debuted PLAYBOX, its award-winning competitive socialising concept, designed to bring groups together through immersive gaming, entertainment, and shared experiencesfurther solidifying BOXPARKs position at the forefront of leisure innovation.

BOXPARKs rapid growth and forward-thinking approach have earned it a place on The Sunday Times Virgin Atlantic Fast Track 100 three times, along with a host of prestigious industry accolades including the Retail Week Awards, MAPIC Awards, Global RLI Awards, New London Awards, RIBA Awards, and Revo Gold Awards.

With a continued commitment to building vibrant, community-led destinations, BOXPARK remains a leader in the UKs lifestyle, hospitality, and entertainment landscape

organizationDescription:
Pizza Wine is a highly crushable, canned sparkling red wine thats great with pizza and great without. Made from hybrid grapes grown in NYs Lake Erie AVA, Pizza Wine is on a mission to make wine less intimidating without sacrificing quality. 

Our grapes are farmed with minimal intervention. Nothing is added that doesnt belong (no artificial flavors, no concentrates, no natural flavors, no chemicals). Every seed, skin, stem is returned to the soil. 

Were restaurant and bar people (not celebrities) that have built our careers around making people feel welcome. We live for the perfect pairing and are devastated (truly) that traditional wine culture is pushing people away. 

We could spend hours explaining why this pizza and wine pairing works  how the structure perfectly balances the acidity in the tomatoes, and the bubbles attach to and wash down the fats from the cheese  but thats not our vibe. 

Italians have been drinking sparkling red with pizza for hundreds of years  we couldnt find one that tasted the way we wanted, so we made it ourselves. Drink it from the can, pour it over ice, or mix it with Coke for all we care.  Hope you love it

organizationDescription:
Barbers Cheesemakers has been making award-winning cheddar cheese since 1833, longer than anyone else in the world. In 1712, the Barbers started farming lands in Somerset, and in 1833 Daniel Barber started making cheddar cheese. Over seven generations, the Barber family has built up the business, to include Ford Farm Cheesemakers, too. 

Barbers supply cheese throughout Britain as well as internationally, winning many awards along the way. This year Barbers scooped a total of 2 Trophies, 11 Golds, 10 Silvers and 11 Bronzes at the International Cheese Awards, World Cheese Awards, Global Cheese Awards and South West Cheese Awards.

Barbers source their milk from either their own cows, or from local dairy farms within a 35 mile radius. The Barbers cows are all grass-fed and stay out in pasture for an average of 224 days each year, and in the winter months eat the home-grown silage. The grass-fed diet makes all the difference to the flavours and quality of the cheese.

The starter cultures that are used also help to make the cheeses exceptional, and give them their unique taste. Barbers are proud to be the sole guardians of the countrys last remaining authentic cheese starter cultures. Nearly 200 years old, the cultures have been refined over the generations and are now protected in their specialist laboratory.

Central to the Barbers principles is sustainability across the farming and across the cheesemaking. Barbers cultivate the wildlife in the fields and orchards, and regulate the soil and drainage to reduce flooding. Within the cheese factory, Barbers are committed to ensuring they reduce environmental impact through following Zero Waste policies. Every part of the milk is used in the cheesemaking processes, and the water is cleaned and buffered using Barbers own natural membrane filtration system.

We at Barbers believe that by taking care of our land, animals and surroundings, we will be rewarded with the very best cheddar now and in the future.
organizationSpecialities: cheddar , wensleydale , vintage cheddar, mature cheddar, mellow cheddar, red leicester, cheddarmakers, cheesemakers, pdo cheddar, cheese starter culture, sustainable farming, sustainable cheesemaking, zero waste, sustainable, grass fed dairy, grass fed cheese, award winning, award winning cheese, handmade cheese, and handmade cheddar

organizationDescription:
Welcome to the electrifying world of Goodness Brewing Co! Founded in 2019 by the dynamic quartet of Damien Legg, Mike Stirling, Joe Louis Sheasgreen, and Zack Ahmed, our journey began with humble homebrewing roots in the west London area of Wood Green. Fast forward to present, weve turned dreams into reality by acquiring our own brick-and-mortar brewery and a spacious taproom that pulses with energy and creativity.

organizationDescription:
Barbers Cheesemakers has been making award-winning cheddar cheese since 1833, longer than anyone else in the world. In 1712, the Barbers started farming lands in Somerset, and in 1833 Daniel Barber started making cheddar cheese. Over seven generations, the Barber family has built up the business, to include Ford Farm Cheesemakers, too. 

Barbers supply cheese throughout Britain as well as internationally, winning many awards along the way. This year Barbers scooped a total of 2 Trophies, 11 Golds, 10 Silvers and 11 Bronzes at the International Cheese Awards, World Cheese Awards, Global Cheese Awards and South West Cheese Awards.

Barbers source their milk from either their own cows, or from local dairy farms within a 35 mile radius. The Barbers cows are all grass-fed and stay out in pasture for an average of 224 days each year, and in the winter months eat the home-grown silage. The grass-fed diet makes all the difference to the flavours and quality of the cheese.

The starter cultures that are used also help to make the cheeses exceptional, and give them their unique taste. Barbers are proud to be the sole guardians of the countrys last remaining authentic cheese starter cultures. Nearly 200 years old, the cultures have been refined over the generations and are now protected in their specialist laboratory.

Central to the Barbers principles is sustainability across the farming and across the cheesemaking. Barbers cultivate the wildlife in the fields and orchards, and regulate the soil and drainage to reduce flooding. Within the cheese factory, Barbers are committed to ensuring they reduce environmental impact through following Zero Waste policies. Every part of the milk is used in the cheesemaking processes, and the water is cleaned and buffered using Barbers own natural membrane filtration system.

We at Barbers believe that by taking care of our land, animals and surroundings, we will be rewarded with the very best cheddar now and in the future.

organizationDescription:
Founded in 2008 by Jade Holland Cooper, Holland Cooper is a modern expression of British heritage. Born from Jades unique blend of rural upbringing, fashion lineage, and entrepreneurial spirit, the brand was created to fill a distinct gap in the market for contemporary tweed wearuniting timeless style with British craftsmanship and a deep connection to country living.

What began with 30 mini-skirts at a modest stand at Badminton Horse Trials has since evolved into a globally recognised luxury lifestyle brand for women and men. Today, our collections span bespoke tailoring, outerwear, equestrian-wear, casual-wear, and morecrafted for a discerning customer who lives confidently between the countryside and the city.

At the heart of Holland Cooper is our tailoring: designed in the Cotswolds and brought to life in heritage mills across Yorkshire. Every garment is hand-cut and stitched using time-honoured techniques, with fabrics sourced from family-run mills that have been operating for over a century. As one of the UKs leading buyers of British tweed and wool, we transform heritage materials into elevated, enduring icons of British design.

From trench coats and expertly tailored suits to equestrian staples and investment outerwear, our pieces are built for those who live with intention and dress with purpose.

With a growing retail presence, international expansion, and the launch of our new 70,000 sq ft HQ, Holland Cooper continues to evolve while staying true to our roots. Our founder Jade remains closely involved in every facet of the businessfrom product development to customer connectionensuring the brand continues to champion British manufacturing, impeccable quality, and a curated lifestyle at scale

organizationDescription:
Green House Group was founded in 2009. In the years since we have grown with the platforms, technology, trends, and our clients, to delivery an all-round digital support service for artists, music brands and promoters at any level.

We worked with Charli XCX from her first video going live; seen chart success with The Specials, M, Simple Minds, Suede, Laura Marling, Frank Carter & The Rattlesnakes, and The Kooks; helped The Libertines reunite to headline a 65,000 capacity sold out crowd in Hyde Park; launched the critically acclaimed Rockaway Beach festival - which we also co-programme; modernised how catalogue artists sell to their fans; seen sold out capacity crowds at Secret Garden Party, End of the Road, 2000trees, ArcTanGent, Liverpool International Music Festival, and Barn on the Farm festivals; and so much more.

Weve worked with artists over a broad spectrum of genres and decades, from alternative icons like Gang of Four and Echo & The Bunnymen, to breaking acts Barns Courtney and The Marmozets, to rock behemoths Pixies and The Zombies, and pop legends Shakin Stevens, Michael Bolton, Simply Red, and The Thompson Twins.

Recently weve been working with the Bloc Party, Music Venue Trust, Key Production, Trojan Records, World Circuit Records, BMG, Universal, Sony, Chrysalis Records, and CMU. 

Our backgrounds range from PR and journalism, to making music, event promotion, and illustration, giving us an unparalleled understanding of the entire entertainment industry and the needs of our clients. 

Members of Green House Group have acted as advisors to PRS, been guest university lecturers, and are regular panelists at conferences such as The Great Escape, Sound & Vision, Wide Days and SXSW. Outside of this were music makers, DJs, presenters, writers, and promoters. 

We are a versatile team, who are equally at home out in the field (literally) working hands-on with you, as we are at our desks getting the job done

organizationDescription:
About The Great Pitch Company
The Great Pitch Company helps agencies win more of the opportunities that matterand do so more consistently.
Founded in 2019, the company works with agencies of all sizesfrom independents to global holding groupssupporting them in one of the most critical and resource-intensive areas of their business: pitching.
In many organisations, significant time, cost and effort go into new business, yet outcomes remain inconsistent. The Great Pitch Company focuses on the moments that determine successhow clearly the proposition is defined, how effectively the story is told, and how well teams perform under pressure.
Its work is structured across two complementary divisions:
Great Now  working alongside agency teams on live pitches and re-pitches to improve performance and increase conversion rates in real time.
Great Forever  building long-term capability through training and consultancy, aligning people, culture and process to help agencies win more business and waste less resource.
With team members across the UK, Italy, Switzerland and the US, The Great Pitch Company operates internationally, supporting agencies across a wide range of disciplines.
The company combines this hands-on work with industry insight. In 2021 it launched The Great Pitch Poll, an annual survey of business development practices across leading UK agencies, examining performance, process and the impact of pitching on areas such as mental wellbeing.
Recent results include a 100% pitch conversion rate across supported projects in 2024, and in 2025 helping clients secure over $1.5 billion in media billings

Specially designed to fit and flatter swollen feet, our footwear is much wider, deeper and roomier than the wide-fitting footwear youll find on the high street. All our styles have the same extra roomy width fitting (6E for women and 3H for men) and can be adjusted to fit a range of swelling and foot shapes so theres no need to worry about complicated foot measurements. 

Based in the South West of England, we have over 40 years experience in fitting swollen feet and legs. Our footwear is made by experienced craftspeople who mix traditional skills with the latest technology. We use premium quality materials along with state-of-the-art foot comfort to give our customers the most comfortable footwear possible. Thanks to innovations such as our Hidden Depth design, Cosyfeet fits swollen feet with ease but still looks stylish and streamlined. Some styles can also fit bunions, problem toes, bandaging and orthotics. 

We also offers a range of specialist socks and hosiery. Our extra roomy collection is designed to fit swollen feet and legs while our diabetic-friendly socks wont rub or irritate vulnerable feet. 

At Cosyfeet, were passionate about providing the best products and services possible, which is why thousands of people shop with us and we are recommended by UK healthcare professionals nationwide. We are also recognised by the Healthy Footwear Guide, a collaboration between UK footwear brands and foot health professionals

organizationDescription:
SHOW was founded by Peter Howarth, formerly editor-in-chief of Arena and Esquire magazines, Ian Pendleton (Royal College of Art and Esquire) and Joanne Glasbey (The Economist and Esquire). All three are directors of the company and met while Peter was editor-in-chief of Esquire, Ian was art director and Joanne deputy editor. They set up Show Media as a publishing company in 2003 with the idea of producing both private magazines for luxury brands and consumer titles for other publishing firms.

After a couple of years, clients were asking SHOW to create brochures, adverts, logos and other printed marketing collateral, websites and apps. From there, the company has grown into a full-service creative agency, specialising in content creation for different print and digital platforms. We work with a wide range of clients from luxury fashion and jewellery houses to property companies and premium drinks brands.

We care deeply about creativity and flawless execution, and value working with
brands that share our passion. With our editorial background, the inspiration behind each of our projects is storytelling  how to effectively tell our clients stories in a way that will inspire and engage.

Our services include:
 Magazine production
 Creative direction
 Editing and copywriting
 Design and branding
 Shoot production
 Print management
 Translation services
 Digital content
 Web design and build
 Social media management
 Digital newsletters

organizationDescription:
republiqe  The Future of Fashion is Digital 

At republiqe, we are redefining fashion for the digital age. As the worlds first fully digital fashion house, we create high-quality, wearable digital garments that empower individuals to express themselves across gaming, social media, and the metaversewith zero waste and infinite creativity.

Why Digital Fashion?

 A $5B Industry by 2030  Virtual fashion is no longer a concept; its a movement.
 Sustainable & Scalable  No fabric, no overproduction, no landfilljust limitless design potential.
 The New Retail Frontier  Virtual clothing is being bought, sold, and worn inside digital worlds.

What We Do:

 Wearable Digital Fashion  Designed for gaming, AR, social media, and the metaverse.
 Virtual Retail Activations  Helping brands enter and monetise the metaverse.
 Metaverse Collaborations  Bringing global brands into digital spaces like Roblox.

Introducing HEX  The Virtual Shopping Mall
Were not just creating digital fashionwere revolutionizing how its sold. Hex is a first-of-its-kind virtual shopping mall inside Roblox, giving brands a new platform to sell digital wearables, engage customers, and build immersive retail experiences. Brands like Moschino, Irregular Choice, Hype, and Phat Kandi are already on boardis yours?

Lets Build the Future of Fashion

We work with forward-thinking brands, designers, and innovators who want to be part of the next retail revolution. Whether youre looking to launch a digital fashion collection, create an interactive metaverse store, or explore new revenue streams, republiqe is your partner in digital fashion innovation.

 Get in touch to explore the future of fashion.
